BQ.name: Caribbean Netherlands
BQ.notes: The Caribbean Netherlands is under the separate Opiumwet 1960 BES: cannabis cultivation and possession are prohibited, with narrow lawful medical/professional possession exceptions for legally obtained medicines.
BQ.reason: BES law is strict and separate from the European Netherlands tolerance model; only narrow medical/professional exceptions are supported.

BV.name: Bouvet Island
BV.notes: Bouvet Island is a Norwegian dependency; Norwegian criminal law applies to Norwegian jurisdiction areas and Norway allows cannabis medicines only through approved products or named-patient/special-permit pathways.
BV.reason: Apply Norway-derived status: recreational illegal, medical access limited to regulated/special-permit pathways.

CC.name: Cocos (Keeling) Islands
CC.notes: Western Australian-style drug prohibitions apply in the Cocos (Keeling) Islands, while Australian medicinal cannabis is lawful through prescription/TGA pathways.
CC.reason: Recreational use is illegal; medicinal cannabis follows Australia's regulated prescription framework.

CX.name: Christmas Island
CX.notes: Christmas Island follows Australian external-territory/Western Australian drug-law settings: recreational use is prohibited, while medicinal cannabis is legal through Australian prescription/TGA pathways.
CX.reason: Recreational use is illegal; medicinal cannabis follows Australia's regulated prescription framework.

GF.name: French Guiana
GF.notes: French Guiana follows French law: recreational cannabis is prohibited; medical cannabis has only a controlled/transition access framework for eligible patients.
GF.reason: French overseas department status inherits France's illegal recreational and limited medical framework.

GP.name: Guadeloupe
GP.notes: Guadeloupe follows French law: recreational cannabis is prohibited; medical cannabis has only a controlled/transition access framework for eligible patients.
GP.reason: French overseas department status inherits France's illegal recreational and limited medical framework.

LS.name: Lesotho
LS.notes: Recreational use and unlicensed cultivation remain prohibited; Lesotho licenses medical-grade cannabis cultivation/processing/export under a controlled industry model.
LS.reason: The lawful signal is licensed medical/scientific/export cultivation, not general adult use; strict prohibition remains outside licenses.

MH.name: Marshall Islands
MH.notes: Cannabis possession is prohibited under the Narcotic Drugs (Prohibition and Control) Act 1987; a prescription exception exists in statute, but no medicinal cannabis availability/program is confirmed on the islands.
MH.reason: The statutory prescription language does not establish available medical cannabis access; possession penalties make enforcement strict.

MQ.name: Martinique
MQ.notes: Martinique follows French law: recreational cannabis is prohibited; medical cannabis has only a controlled/transition access framework for eligible patients.
MQ.reason: French overseas department status inherits France's illegal recreational and limited medical framework.

PH.name: Philippines
PH.notes: Recreational cannabis, cultivation and sale remain prohibited under the Dangerous Drugs Act; medical access is limited to FDA compassionate special permits for serious or terminal illness/unregistered drug products.
PH.reason: Strict general prohibition remains; the verified medical signal is a narrow compassionate special-permit path.

RE.name: Reunion
RE.notes: Reunion follows French law: recreational cannabis is prohibited; medical cannabis has only a controlled/transition access framework for eligible patients.
RE.reason: French overseas department status inherits France's illegal recreational and limited medical framework.

SJ.name: Svalbard and Jan Mayen
SJ.notes: Norwegian criminal law applies on Svalbard, and Norwegian criminal legislation also covers Jan Mayen; Norway permits cannabis medicines only through approved products or named-patient/special-permit pathways.
SJ.reason: Apply Norway-derived status: recreational illegal, medical access limited to regulated/special-permit pathways.

TK.name: Tokelau
TK.notes: Tokelau Customs Regulations define cannabis plant material as a prohibited plant; import is an offence, with a narrow exception for products lawfully supplied or prescribed outside Tokelau for medical use.
TK.reason: General cannabis import/use is prohibited; medical status is limited to narrow prescribed-import exceptions.

WS.name: Samoa
WS.notes: Cannabis is prohibited under Samoa's narcotics framework; reform reports discussed medicinal cannabis, but the known medicinal cultivation proposal was rejected and no medical cannabis program is confirmed.
WS.reason: The reform discussion is not enacted medical access; prohibition and penalties keep the status red.

XK.name: Kosovo
XK.notes: Kosovo treats use, possession, production and trafficking of illicit drugs as criminal offences; no public medical cannabis framework is confirmed.
XK.reason: Missing enforcement is resolved by criminal-code penalty/enforcement sources; no medical framework is confirmed.

YT.name: Mayotte
YT.notes: Mayotte follows French law: recreational cannabis is prohibited; medical cannabis has only a controlled/transition access framework for eligible patients.
YT.reason: French overseas department status inherits France's illegal recreational and limited medical framework.

BD.name: Bangladesh
BD.notes: Cannabis cultivation, transport, sale, purchase and possession remain prohibited under Bangladesh narcotics law; later enforcement notes report harsher cannabis enforcement since 2017 and criminal penalties remain in force.
BD.reason: Control audit requires RED: medical access is not confirmed and strict criminal penalties override older lax-enforcement notes.
